text: NVIDIA reports record Q4 revenue of $22.1B, beating estimates by 20%. Data center segment drives growth with AI chip demand surging across cloud providers.
Apple unveils new AI-powered iPhone features at spring event. Analysts raise price targets citing ecosystem lock-in and growing services revenue.
Amazon Web Services announces major expansion into Southeast Asian markets. Cloud infrastructure spending projected to hit $180B globally this year.
Microsoft Azure market share grows to 25%, narrowing gap with AWS. Enterprise AI adoption accelerating across Fortune 500 companies.
Meta Platforms sees Instagram ad revenue surge 35% YoY. Reels monetization now exceeds Stories as Gen-Z engagement metrics soar.
Tesla deliveries miss estimates for second straight quarter. Competition from Chinese EVs intensifies in European markets.
AMD gains server CPU market share from Intel, now at 33%. New MI350 AI chips compete directly with NVIDIA's H200 lineup.
JPMorgan reports strong Q1 with investment banking fees up 45%. Interest income remains robust amid elevated rate environment.
Goldman Sachs warns of commercial real estate stress. $1.2T in CRE loans maturing this year as office vacancy rates hit 20%.
Visa and Mastercard volumes surge as consumer spending remains resilient. Cross-border transaction growth accelerates post-pandemic normalization.
Bank of America sees digital banking adoption hit 75% of customer base. Operational efficiency gains drive cost reduction program.
UnitedHealth Group raises full-year guidance on strong Medicare Advantage enrollment. Healthcare spending growth outpaces GDP for third straight year.
Pfizer's new RSV vaccine receives expanded FDA approval. Pipeline diversity grows with 19 Phase 3 candidates, but COVID revenue decline continues.
Johnson & Johnson completes Shockwave Medical acquisition. Medtech segment now represents 35% of total revenue with strong surgical robotics growth.
Eli Lilly's weight loss drug Zepbound generates $4.5B in quarterly sales. GLP-1 market projected to reach $150B by 2030.
ExxonMobil announces $60B Permian Basin investment plan through 2030. Oil prices stabilize near $78 as OPEC+ maintains production cuts.
NextEra Energy secures $15B in new renewable contracts. Solar and battery storage costs continue declining, boosting clean energy margins.
Chevron faces regulatory headwind in Gulf of Mexico operations. Environmental compliance costs rise 12% amid stricter EPA guidelines.
Procter & Gamble raises prices 5% on household staples. Consumer goods inflation persists despite moderating input costs, margin expansion accelerates.
Coca-Cola sees emerging market volumes rise 8%. Africa and Southeast Asia drive growth as middle class expands and urbanization accelerates.
Home Depot reports stronger-than-expected pro contractor spending. Housing renovation cycle begins as existing home sales show signs of recovery.
Disney+ subscriber growth reignites with ad-supported tier. Theme park revenue hits all-time high as international parks expand capacity.
Netflix's ad-supported plan reaches 60M subscribers. Live sports content including NFL Christmas games boost engagement metrics to record highs.
Federal Reserve signals two rate cuts possible in 2026 second half. Inflation moderating but remains above 2% target at 2.8%.
US GDP growth slows to 1.8% in Q1 preliminary estimate. Consumer spending remains positive but business investment softens amid tariff uncertainty.
Labor market remains tight with unemployment at 3.8%. Wage growth of 3.5% continues to outpace inflation, supporting consumer spending.
10-year Treasury yield stabilizes at 4.1% as bond market prices in gradual easing. Yield curve normalization continues after prolonged inversion.
Gold prices surge to $2,350/oz on geopolitical tensions and central bank buying. Physical demand from Asia reaches multi-year highs.
Oil prices drop 3% as global demand forecasts revised lower. IEA cuts 2026 demand growth estimate amid slowing Chinese industrial activity.
Copper prices hit record highs on AI data center power demand. Electrification trends and supply constraints create multi-year bull case.
VIX volatility index drops below 14 signaling market complacency. Analysts warn low volatility often precedes sharp corrections.
China's manufacturing PMI rises above 50 for first time in six months. Stimulus measures boost factory output and export orders.
European Central Bank cuts rates to 2.75% as eurozone growth stagnates. Euro weakens against dollar, boosting US multinational earnings.
S&P 500 earnings growth accelerates to 12% YoY in Q1. Technology and healthcare sectors lead while materials and utilities lag.
Small-cap Russell 2000 outperforms large-caps as rate cut expectations build. Regional banks and industrials lead the rotation trade.
Broadcom acquires VMware integration proceeds ahead of schedule. Combined entity targets $8.5B in annualized software revenue as cloud pivots.
Palantir wins $980M US Army AI contract. Government AI spending accelerates as defense modernization prioritizes autonomous systems.
CrowdStrike's revenue grows 35% as cybersecurity spend remains mission-critical. Consolidation platform strategy wins enterprise deals from legacy vendors.
Moderna pivots pipeline beyond COVID. RSV and cancer vaccine candidates show promising Phase 2 data, but profitability timeline extends.
Rising insurance premiums in Florida and California drive property insurance costs up 25%. Climate-related risks reshape housing market dynamics.
Sunbelt migration continues as Texas and Florida add most new housing permits. Affordable Midwest markets see investor interest surge.
Mortgage rates drop to 6.2% from 7.1% peak, spurring refinancing wave. Existing home sales tick up 4% month-over-month.
Remote work normalization drives suburban and secondary city housing demand. Office-to-residential conversions accelerate in major metros.
REIT sector rallies as rate cut expectations grow. Industrial and data center REITs outperform while office REITs continue to struggle.
Walmart expands healthcare clinics to 1,000 locations. Retail healthcare disruption pressures traditional hospital systems on pricing.
Semiconductor supply chain diversification continues. TSMC Arizona fab reaches production milestone as US CHIPS Act funding flows.
Natural gas prices spike 15% on colder-than-expected spring forecast. Utility stocks rally as storage levels drop below five-year average.
Bitcoin ETFs see record $2.4B inflows in single week. Institutional adoption accelerates as major banks launch crypto custody services.
Lululemon faces margin pressure from aggressive promotions. Athletic apparel market saturates as consumer shifts to value brands.
Caterpillar reports record backlog for construction and mining equipment. Infrastructure spending bill creates multi-year tailwind for industrial stocks.
